findings.evidence: `rg` shows only `leftTensorIdeal_sup`/`rightTensorIdeal_sup` in this file, while Mathlib provides `Ideal.map_iSup` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Maps.lean:236` and the local lattice pattern includes `Subcomodule.map_iSup` at `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcomodule/Lattice.lean:261`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] theorem leftTensorIdeal_iSup {ι : Sort*} (I : ι → Ideal H) : leftTensorIdeal (R := R) (H := H) (⨆ i, I i) = ⨆ i, leftTensorIdeal (R := R) (H := H) (I i)` and the analogous right theorem using `Ideal.map_iSup`; derive or keep the binary lemmas as special cases.
findings.issue: `leftTensorIdeal_sup` and `rightTensorIdeal_sup` only state binary join preservation, while the same PR adds arbitrary Hopf-ideal suprema at lines 469 and 483; this leaves the tensor-ideal API under-generalized.

summary: The Hopf-ideal lattice itself is generalized to arbitrary suprema, but the tensor-ideal compatibility API remains only binary. At this level the natural statement is the `iSup`/`sSup` preservation theorem, with binary `sup` as a specialization.
